original_hypothesis.title: Malicious Operator Can Exhaust User Lock Slots
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Operators have the privilege to call `stakeFor(account, amount, true)` to create locked positions for any user. Because the contract enforces a strict `maxLocks` limit per user, a malicious or compromised operator can repeatedly call `stakeFor` with the `minLockAmount` to completely fill a victim's lock slots.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Once a user's lock slots are filled, they are blocked from locking any of their own tokens until the operator-created locks expire. This effectively griefs their ability to earn boosted yields on larger deposits.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (lockCount == maxLocks) revert ErrMaxUserLocksExceeded() applies to operator-initiated locks

preconditions: Operator key is compromised or operator acts maliciously
User has fewer than maxLocks active

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Operator calls stakeFor(victim, minLockAmount, true) across multiple epochs
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, purely a griefing/DoS attack against specific users

economic_questions: What is the cost to the operator to execute this griefing?
Can the user bypass this by using a different address?
validation_requirements: Execute stakeFor as operator until maxLocks is reached
Attempt to call stake(amount, true) as the user and verify revert

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Requires multiple epochs to create distinct locks

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Targeted denial of service for yield farming

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of boosted yield on their intended deposits

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify if multiple stakeFor calls in the same epoch consume multiple slots (they don't, they add to the same slot, so attack requires waiting across epochs)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
